untranslatable_reason: Signal requires explicit IV (Implied Volatility) percentile, realized volatility comparison, and gamma/theta Greeks tracking. These are options-specific Greeks and volatility metrics not available in price-action indicators. hvol_20 (historical volatility) and india_vix (volatility index) are proxies but cannot accurately replicate IV percentile thresholds or gamma/theta dynamics. The core signal depends on options Greeks which require real-time IV surface data, not available in standard OHLCV backtesting.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy mapping: hvol_20 vs india_vix comparison used to infer IV regime (high/low), with india_vix thresholds as volatility regime filters. However, this is a structural translation that loses the precise gamma-theta alignment logic and IV percentile calculations central to the original signal. This rule is fundamentally unsuitable for price-action-only backtesting without options market microstructure data.
